# 

# **BuildParty PRD \- MVP / V1 / V2 / V3**

# **Purpose of this document**

This document is the core product requirements document for BuildParty.

It has two jobs.

First, it is an **orientation document**. Anyone joining the project — designer, engineer, marketer, strategist, operator, investor, or advisor — should be able to read the beginning of this document and understand what BuildParty is, why it matters, why now is the right moment, what problem it solves, who it serves, how it differs from current tools, and what kind of company it is trying to become.

Second, it is an **execution document**. It defines the staged product roadmap across: \- MVP \- V1 \- V2 \- V3

It is intended to support: \- product design \- front-end engineering \- back-end engineering \- AI / agent engineering \- growth and marketing \- creator / community strategy \- pricing and monetization \- sponsorship design \- analytics and measurement \- fundraising and GTM narrative

The document should therefore be read as both: \- a company thesis \- a staged product plan

---

# **1\. Executive Narrative Context**

## **1.1 What is BuildParty?**

BuildParty is the live platform where AI builder communities launch, demo, learn, and build together.

It is a new live, agent-native layer for AI builders, AI creators, AI communities, and AI tool providers who need a live, agent-native platform rather than only static content, async chat channels, webinars, or recorded videos. BuildParty is for builders and organizations that thrive with a live environment for product launches, technical demos, creator-led teaching, collaborative build sessions, and community interaction can happen in one coherent system.

BuildParty combines several things fragmented across multiple products:

* Live demos (Zoom/Youtube Live)  
* Async launch channels (ProductHunt)  
* Collaborative build sessions (Zoom)  
* Creator-led courses & camps (Maven (live), Udemy/Coursera (recorded)  
* PreParties & AfterParties (non-existent)  
* Live community discussion (rare, Zoom)  
* Live audience participation (non-existent)  
* AI agents that host, remember, guide, demo, summarize, and amplify the experience (limited)

The strategic positioning is:

**BuildParty is the live infrastructure for AI builder communities.**

And, from the platform / company angle:

**BuildParty is the live Builder Engagement, Activation, and creator interaction layer for AI platforms and communities.**

That positioning matters. BuildParty is not trying to be another generic community product, generic course product, or generic webinar product. It is trying to define a category around live, agent-native builder engagement.

---

## **1.2 Why BuildParty is needed**

Today, the AI builder / creator stack is fragmented.

Creators use:

* Zoom and non-agent native webinar platforms for live sessions  
* YouTube Live for broadcasts and recorded discovery  
* Skool, Circle, Mighty Networks, and others for creator communities & courses  
* Discord & Slack for asynchronous community “show & tell”  
*  Product Hunt, GitHub, X, for recorded launches, clips, and discovery

These tools do not all fail in the same way, and they should not be treated as if they are interchangeable.

### **Discord and Slack are complements, not replacements**

Discord and Slack are entrenched and useful async coordination layers. They are good for:

* ongoing chat  
* community coordination  
* lightweight showcases  
* support thread  
* async discussion

BuildParty is not intended to replace them, but to complement them with a live experience they don’t provide.

### **Zoom and current live delivery tools are the more direct targets**

For many builder communities and creator-led programs, Zoom and similar live tools are much closer to what BuildParty is trying to improve on or displace.

They are functional, but they were not designed for an agent-native world in which: \- builders want to show what they launched live \- attendees want to interact directly with builders in real time \- creators want live communities rather than passive classes \- sessions should turn into persistent memory, progress, and amplification assets \- AI agents should operate before, during, and after the session

### **Skool & other creator/community products are lacking and not agent-native**

Skool is made for creators, and clearly serves a market for solopreneur creators.. But its live experience is non-existent and not builder-native to what BuildParty can become for AI builders and AI creators. BuildParty is not “Skool plus a little better live infrastructure.” The stronger framing is:

**Today’s creator/community infrastructure was not built for an agent-native world. BuildParty is.**

### **Core unmet need 1**

### **Live launch and demo infrastructure for AI builders**

One of the clearest unmet needs is not just that “builders want a place to show what they built.” That is too generic and slightly misleading. The sharper need is this:

There is an explosion of AI products, AI startups, and AI tools being built and launched by established and new startups, and solopreneurs. Those builders and companies need a live place to:

* demo live what they just launched  
* explain what the product does  
* explain their stack and how they built it  
* answer questions from a highly engaged, relevant audience  
* gain visibility, feedback, and social proof

This is especially important because the AI tool market moves extremely quickly. Builders and companies benefit from live launch moments, not just static launch pages (ProductHunt) or isolated recorded demo videos (LinkedIn/X posts, Youtube)

### **Core unmet need 2:**

### Builders want live learning, explanation & interaction, not just passive content

Builders do not only want polished pitch videos or short launch clips.

They want to watch the builder to explain:

* what was built  
* how it works  
* what tools and stack were used  
* what tradeoffs and constraints existed  
* how it fits into current workflows

Then, after that explanation, they want:

* live questions  
* direct conversation  
* technical clarification  
* collaborative engagement

So the point is not that people log in primarily to ask questions. The primary behavior is: 

1\. I watch the builder explain the build live

2\. I understand the stack / workflow / context \- miguel stack panel

3\. I then ask questions or collaborate or replicate/fork

That sequence matters for product design and messaging.

### **Core unmet need 3**

### **Creators need live community infrastructure for an agent-native world**

AI creators are exploding with building communities for:

* Classes, camps. cohort courses & workshops  
* Office hours  
* Demos  
* Community sessions

These experiences are economically important, but the live layer is still often weak.

The problem is not merely that they need something “10–20% better than Zoom.”

The problem is that the current live layer was not built for an agent-native world.

BuildParty serves creators by making live sessions:

* more interactive  
* more community-centered  
* more agent-mediated  
* more persistent across time  
* more amplifiable into clips, recaps, and posts  
* more compatible with student demos, after-parties, and follow-on collaboration

### 

### **Core unmet need 4:**

### **Developer Relations is already becoming something else**

The legacy term “Developer Relations” is still used, but the functional reality is shifting.

Historically, DevRel meant a mix of:

* Documentation  
* blog posts  
* Webinars  
* Conferences  
* Hackathons  
* Office hours  
* Chat communities

Many of these still matter. Hackathons, in particular, are real and useful. But the old frame is too narrow for what AI platforms now need.

A better framing for the modern requirement is:

**Builder Engagement, Builder Learning, Builder Activation, and Builder Programming**

AI platforms increasingly need:

* live demo and launch sessions  
* recurring learning sessions  
* live build sessions  
* creator / expert-led educational programming  
* agent-mediated event operations and follow-up  
* social amplification tied directly to what happened live

BuildParty is designed to become the live software layer for that new model.

### **Core unmet need 5**

### **Current live tools are not structured for agent-managed community programming**

The more strategic BuildParty becomes, the less it should be understood as “webinars with AI sprinkled on top.”

The real shift is that BuildParty can eventually support:

* round-the-clock live programming  
* platform-specific builder & cobuild sessions  
* launch/demo sessions  
* BuildBuddy sessions  
* Creator classes, bootcamps and after-parties  
* agent-driven event creation and marketing  
* agent-generated clips and social posts  
* smart tagging of presenters and top contributors

This is a materially different product from a generic webinar platform.

---

## 

## **1.3 Why now**

AI is rapidly changing how work is done, how software is built, how companies structure themselves, and how labor markets behave.

Major employers are now openly citing AI as a reason to run smaller, more productive teams. Block (market cap: $38B), just laid off 4,000 employees while explicitly citing AI s, and investors sent the stock sharply higher after the announcement. ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/business/dorseys-blunt-ai-warning-sharpens-debate-over-jobs-profits-2026-02-27/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

The broader labor market is also being reshaped. The World Economic Forum’s Future of Jobs Report 2025, based on more than 1,000 employers representing over 14 million workers, concludes that AI and related technologies will materially reshape jobs and skills through 2030\. ([weforum.org](https://www.weforum.org/publications/the-future-of-jobs-report-2025/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

This is especially acute for:

* recent graduates  
* current students  
* junior developers  
* early-career knowledge workers  
* solopreneurs and creators trying to stay relevant

Recent reporting also points to increasing risk and anxiety around entry-level knowledge work and junior technical roles, where AI can already perform large portions of the work. ([wsj.com](https://www.wsj.com/economy/jobs/ai-entry-level-job-impact-5c687c84?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

This creates a massive and immediate demand for:

* AI education  
* live builder learning  
* practical demonstrations  
* guided skill development  
* rapid adaptation

The opportunity is not only AI tooling. One of the largest markets emerging from this moment is:

**Builder learning, builder activation, and creator-led AI education**

People do not simply need more written content or more recordings. They need

* live learning  
* practical demos  
* live Q\&A  
* guided build sessions  
* a view into what others are building now  
* systems that create momentum and follow-through

BuildParty is designed for this moment.

---

## **1.4 BuildParty as product \+ programming network**

BuildParty should be understood as both:

1. a software service  
2. a programming and activation network

### The software provides:

* the live stage  
* the agenda  
* the interaction rails (chat, Q\&A, polls)  
* the green room  
* creator mode  
* session memory (for participants & creators)  
* the AI agent layer  
* the recap and social amplification systems

### The programming network provides:

* Launch Days  
* Demo Days & Demo Nights  
* Mainstage co-watch events  
* Creator-led courses  
* CoBuild sessions  
* Buddy sessions  
* Platform-specific build sessions (e.g. LiveKit, Langchain, Claude)  
* Recurring builder/community programming

This distinction matters because BuildParty should not be designed as a static product with isolated features. It should be designed as software that enables a durable, recurring, live builder network.

---

## **1.5 LiveKit and the BuildParty thesis**

BuildParty is built on top of LiveKit.

LiveKit provides the enabling real-time layer for:

* live audio/video  
* multimodal experiences  
* agents as participants and hosts in rooms  
* much richer session UI patterns than ordinary webinar tools

BuildParty uses that layer to create:

* live builder demo sessions  
* creator-led live communities  
* collaborative build rooms  
* Agent-hosted and AI-assisted sessions

Strategically, BuildParty can become:

* showcase application for LiveKit  
* a strong candidate for LiveKit ecosystem programming and education  
* a powerful example of live builder activation built on LiveKit’s realtime stack  
* a LiveKit-based builder engagement and activation platform

But BuildParty is not a LiveKit marketing page. It is an independent product that uses LiveKit as the enabling real-time layer.

---

## **1.6 Why this narrative matters for the team**

The design, engineering, GTM, pricing, analytics, sponsorships, and community strategy for BuildParty should all be informed by the fact that BuildParty is a response to:

* exploding AI learning demand  
* creator-led education growth  
* evolving builder culture  
* the need to transform DevRel into Builder Engagement / Activation  
* dramatic labor-market change

BuildParty is not just a collection of features. It is a product thesis about what live AI builder infrastructure should become.

---

# **2\. Product Definition**

BuildParty is the live platform where AI builder communities launch, demo, and build together.

BuildParty combines:

* live builder demos  
* creator-led courses  
* collaborative build sessions  
* launch showcases  
* Competitions  
* live community interaction  
* Agent-native session infrastructure

BuildParty is positioned as:

* the live infrastructure for AI builder communities  
* the live Builder Engagement / Activation and creator interaction layer for AI platforms and communities

---

# 3\. Core User Types

## Builders

* ## AI developers

* ## AI solopreneurs

* ## AI tool creators

* ## AI startup founders

* ## AI experimental builders and hackers

## Creators

* AI YouTube creators  
* AI newsletter publishers  
* AI cohort/course creators  
* AI educators

## Communities / Platforms

* ## AI builder communities

* ## AI platforms / devtool ecosystems

* ## platform teams responsible for builder learning and activation

## Internal Roles

* ## host / moderator

* ## session producer

* ## creator / presenter

* ## community manager

---

# 4\. Core Product Modes

These are the main BuildParty product modes and they should remain consistent across the roadmap.

* **Watch** \- discover and attend live sessions  
* **CoBuild** \- collaborative live builder rooms  
* **BuddyBuild** \- pair / small-group build support and matching  
* **Courses** \- creator-led cohort bootcamps and class sessions  
* **Launch** \- ritualized showcase for what builders launched or updated  
* **Demo** \- live showcase and explanation of what was built and how it works  
* **Arena** \- live competitions and build challenges  
* **Mainstage** \- co-watch / keynote / major announcement mode

---

# 5\. Product Principles

1\.    	**Live-first, not content-first**

2\.    	**Builders interact, not just watch**

3\.    	**Creators host communities, not just webinars**

4\.    	**Agents are product-native, not decorative**

5\.    	**The product should feel premium, not like generic webinar software**

6\.    	**Communities and platforms are core customers, not an afterthought**

7\.    	**The MVP must validate behavior, not just software polish**

8\.    	**Analytics are product-native, not bolted on later**

---

# 6\. Analytics and Measurement Philosophy

Analytics are a first-class requirement in BuildParty.

Because BuildParty sits at the intersection of builder learning, creator communities, and platform activation, measurement must support multiple layers of value.

## 6.1 Core analytics categories

### Session engagement analytics

Track: \- RSVPs \- attendance \- retention by minute \- chat activity \- Q\&A participation \- poll participation \- on-stage participation \- replay engagement

### Builder activation analytics

Track: \- demo submissions \- launches shown \- co-build participation \- buddy participation \- repeat participation over time \- conversion from watcher to presenter / builder participant

### Creator success analytics

Track: \- creator session attendance \- repeat attendance in creator programs \- course progression \- after-party participation \- recap / clip usage \- social amplification performance

### Community / platform analytics

Track: \- platform-specific session demand \- builder activation by community or platform \- recurring session attendance \- launch/demo frequency \- creator and builder participation across recurring programs

### Agent analytics

Track: \- Nova usage and outcomes \- Echo recap generation and usage \- Orbit engagement and support effectiveness \- agent-triggered social post generation \- question clustering / moderation quality

### Revenue and monetization analytics

Track: \- subscriptions \- creator revenue \- sponsorship performance \- platform / partner spend \- course conversion \- cohort revenue \- session-to-revenue attribution

## 6.2 Why analytics matter strategically

BuildParty must be able to prove: \- session quality \- builder activation \- creator ROI \- platform ROI \- superiority versus generic webinar tools \- monetization performance

Analytics must therefore be incorporated into product architecture from the beginning.

---

# 7\. Key Product Problems to Solve

1\.    	Builders lack a live, persistent place to launch and demo their work with real-time feedback.

2\.    	Builders want to see how things were actually built and ask questions directly.

3\.    	Creators need agent-native live infrastructure for AI communities.

4\.    	Communities and AI platforms need a better way to run live builder activation.

5\.    	Users need persistent memory, recap, and progress support across sessions.

---

# 8\. Roadmap Table

| Stage | Product Goal | Primary Users | Core User Promise | Included Modes | Not Yet Included | Success Criteria | Product Risk |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **MVP** | Validate that builders, creators, and early communities want a live, agent-native interaction layer | Builders, creators, early communities | “This is a meaningfully better live builder experience than Zoom/YouTube/Discord alone.” | Watch, Launch/Demo, limited Q\&A, basic creator sessions | Arena, advanced CoBuild, matching, deep analytics products, platform hubs, monetization automation | repeated session attendance, demo submissions, creator interest, usable recap value | too event-like, not enough differentiated product value |
| **V1** | Turn successful sessions into a repeatable product with clear session types and creator use cases | Builders, creators, community operators | “I can reliably host, attend, and learn in live builder sessions here.” | Watch, Launch, Demo, Courses, Mainstage, basic CoBuild, visible agents | advanced Arena, enterprise admin, deeper agent orchestration, marketplace | weekly recurring sessions, creator retention, better session UX, first paid pilots | product becomes fragmented or too broad |
| **V2** | Expand into true builder workflow platform with collaboration and creator monetization | Builders, creators, platforms | “I can learn, build, collaborate, and grow my audience/community here.” | Watch, Launch, Demo, Courses, CoBuild, Buddy, Mainstage, early Arena | full enterprise suite, advanced analytics products, external ecosystem APIs | creator monetization, buddy/co-build retention, higher engagement per user, ecosystem adoption | collaboration complexity, operational burden |
| **V3** | Become live infrastructure for AI builder communities and platform ecosystems | Builders, creators, communities, AI platforms | “This is the live layer for AI builder communities and platform activation.” | Full mode set including platform-specific hubs, advanced agent layer, creator/community operating system | N/A | multiple ecosystem customers, scalable community infrastructure, strong retention and monetization | too many personas, platform complexity |

---

# 9\. Detailed PRD by Stage

## MVP

### MVP Goal

The goal of MVP is for AI creators and builders to join in live, interactive, agent-native sessions to launch, demo, and discuss builds.

The MVP is **not** trying to prove the entire BuildParty vision.

It is trying to validate: \- live attendance demand \- demo supply \- creator session demand \- persistent value from recap / memory \- superiority over generic tools like Zoom \+ Discord \+ YouTube for the live layer

---

### MVP Core User Stories

#### *Builder*

·  	As a builder, I want to attend a live session where another builder shows what they launched or built.

·  	As a builder, I want to hear how they built it and what tools they used.

·  	As a builder, I want to ask questions live after the explanation.

·  	As a builder, I want a recap of what happened so the session creates future value.

#### *Presenter / Creator*

·  	As a presenter, I want to host a live session that feels more interactive than Zoom.

·  	As a creator, I want the audience to participate, not just sit passively.

·  	As a creator, I want session output that can be reused socially after the event.

#### *Community Operator*

·  	As a community host, I want to run a session with agenda, audience interaction, and recap.

---

### MVP Core Features

| Area | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Session page | live session page with agenda, central stage, right-side interaction rail | P0 | based on current WatchParty UI direction |
| Stage | presenter video / screen-share area | P0 | must feel premium and live |
| Agenda | visible structured agenda with session sections | P0 | pre-party, main event, after-party supported |
| Chat | live chat panel | P0 | basic moderation controls |
| Q\&A | audience question queue | P0 | upvote / surface questions |
| Polls | simple live polls | P1 | important for engagement but can be simple in MVP |
| Green room | small backstage / participant strip | P1 | needed for multi-presenter / host workflows |
| Session recap | personalized recap after session | P0 | can be generated via AI |
| AI Host | Nova — AI Host | P0 | host / moderation / timing / transitions |
| Session Memory | Echo — Session Memory | P0 | recap / memory / action items |
| Build Buddy | Orbit — Build Buddy | P1 | light-touch builder assistance |
| Social recap | shareable recap / snippet generation | P1 | important differentiator for creators |
| Mainstage view | co-watch mode for keynote-style events | P1 | supports major announcements and community viewing |
| Core analytics | engagement, attendance, Q\&A, recap usage | P0 | must be instrumented from the start |

---

### MVP Agent Scope

#### *Public-facing agents*

·  	**Nova — AI Host**

·  	**Echo — Session Memory**

#### *Optional / limited MVP exposure*

·  	**Orbit — Build Buddy** in limited settings only

#### *Hidden orchestration*

·  	recap generation

·  	question triage

·  	host prompt flows

·  	session metadata extraction

---

### MVP Non-Goals

Do not build in MVP: \- full creator operating system \- advanced matching / Buddy network \- competitions / Arena logic \- full course management suite \- platform-specific hub admin systems \- complex monetization marketplace \- advanced multi-room spatial experiences \- advanced enterprise analytics dashboards

---

### MVP UX / Product Requirements

1\.    	The session page must feel clearly better than generic Zoom.

2\.    	The interface must visually reinforce:

o   live agenda

o   stage presence

o   audience participation

o   session structure

3\.    	The session recap must feel personalized and useful.

4\.    	The AI Host must feel helpful, not gimmicky.

5\.    	Presenters and communities must be able to lightly brand the session.

---

### MVP Success Metrics

| Metric | Target Signal |
| :---- | :---- |
| Demo submissions / session supply | builders want to present without heavy manual recruiting |
| Session attendance | consistent enough to justify recurring cadence |
| Repeat attendance | attendees come back for multiple sessions |
| Q\&A engagement | live questions / chat signal real participation |
| Recap usefulness | users perceive recap as valuable |
| Creator interest | creators want to host again |
| Community/platform interest | at least early signs of platform-specific nights |

---

### MVP Recommended Build Scope

#### *Front-end*

·  	live session shell

·  	stage view

·  	agenda rail

·  	interaction rail (chat / Q\&A / poll tabs)

·  	green room strip

·  	session-end recap surface

#### *Back-end*

·  	sessions

·  	agendas

·  	users / roles

·  	messages / questions / polls

·  	transcript / recap jobs

·  	media state / room metadata

·  	event analytics collection

#### *Agent layer*

·  	Nova prompting \+ host orchestration

·  	Echo recap pipeline

·  	optional Orbit limited assistance workflow

---

## V1

### V1 Goal

Turn the MVP from a strong live-session prototype into a repeatable product for builders and creators.

The goal for V1 is for BuildParty to become a reliable home for recurring builder sessions and creator-led experiences.

---

### V1 Product Objectives

1\.    	Support multiple recurring session types.

2\.    	Give creators a stronger reason to host on BuildParty.

3\.    	Improve attendee participation and retention.

4\.    	Move beyond “good live session” into “clear product category.”

---

### V1 Session Types

| Session Type | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Launch Day | recurring ritual for showing what builders launched or updated |
| Demo Sessions | focused live demos and discussion |
| Creator Classes | creator-led instructional sessions |
| Mainstage | co-watch / keynote / major announcement sessions |
| Office Hours / AMA | creator or platform support sessions |
| Light CoBuild | live collaborative sessions with simple room mechanics |

---

### V1 Core Features

| Area | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Session templates | reusable templates by session type | P0 | launch day, class, mainstage, AMA |
| Creator mode | branded creator session experience | P0 | creator framing must be visible and premium |
| Session landing pages | session-specific marketing / RSVP pages | P0 | useful for creators and communities |
| Mainstage | polished co-watch mode | P0 | major strategic differentiator |
| Course mode basics | course session UI \+ recap \+ homework / action item framing | P0 | not full LMS yet |
| Better Orbit workflows | more visible Build Buddy support | P1 | especially in CoBuild contexts |
| Session archive | recording \+ recap \+ key resources | P1 | foundation for persistence |
| Role-based controls | host / producer / presenter permissions | P0 | needed for real operations |
| Social amplification | better auto-generated recap clips / posts | P1 | useful for creators |
| Creator analytics | creator session outcomes and engagement | P1 | begins proving creator ROI |

---

### V1 Agent Scope

Public-facing: \- Nova — AI Host \- Echo — Session Memory \- Orbit — Build Buddy

Expanded hidden layer: \- logic review helper for technical sessions \- question summarization \- clip / social post generation \- basic creator companion functions during course sessions

---

### V1 Non-Goals

Do not fully build yet: \- marketplace for templates \- advanced Buddy matching graph \- enterprise control plane \- deep platform-hub analytics products \- full certification stack

---

### V1 Success Metrics

| Metric | Desired Signal |
| :---- | :---- |
| recurring creator sessions | creators host repeat programs |
| session completion | high retention in live sessions |
| recap / archive usage | users revisit session artifacts |
| creator NPS / satisfaction | creators prefer it to Zoom |
| platform-specific session demand | LiveKit-style sessions gain traction |
| social amplification usage | creators share generated outputs |

---

## V2

### V2 Goal

Expand BuildParty into a true builder workflow platform with collaboration and creator monetization.

The goal for V2 is for BuildParty to become the place where builders not only watch and learn, but actively build, collaborate, and grow.

---

### V2 Product Objectives

1\.    	Make collaboration a first-class behavior.

2\.    	Make creator monetization credible and productized.

3\.    	Strengthen the platform value beyond events.

4\.    	Make BuildParty feel like a system, not a collection of modes.

---

### V2 Core Features

| Area | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| CoBuild rooms | stronger collaborative room mechanics | P0 | multiple participants, task flows |
| Buddy matching | matching for paired or small-group build support | P0 | based on interests / tools / needs |
| Creator communities | recurring creator spaces / program shells | P0 | stronger than simple events |
| Course progression | multi-session cohort experience | P0 | progress, recaps, office hours |
| Creator companion | creator-branded companion / Q\&A layer | P1 | key strategic creator differentiator |
| Template hub | reusable session / agent / workflow templates | P1 | supports creator and builder reuse |
| Arena | structured builder competitions | P1 | should now be real, not just placeholder |
| Session identity system | profiles, reputation, builder history | P1 | creates continuity |
| Persistent progress layer | user goals, streaks, ongoing context | P1 | reinforces habit and retention |
| Monetization systems | creator billing, subscriptions, sponsorship support | P0 | needed for durable economics |
| Analytics layer expansion | creator/community/platform dashboards | P1 | must prove value to multiple customer types |

---

### V2 Agent Scope

Public-facing: \- Nova — AI Host \- Echo — Session Memory \- Orbit — Build Buddy \- creator-branded companion instances where needed

Hidden layer expanded: \- buddy matching logic \- logic review / flow-generation tools \- creator knowledge retrieval \- progress coaching / nudges \- session transformation into content / social clips

---

### V2 Success Metrics

| Metric | Desired Signal |
| :---- | :---- |
| co-build participation | users actively join collaborative rooms |
| buddy retention | users return to matched buddy flows |
| course completion | creators can run strong multi-session experiences |
| creator monetization | creators earn revenue on the platform |
| session archive value | BuildParty becomes a persistent knowledge layer |
| user identity / progression | repeat usage builds user history and profile value |

---

## V3

### V3 Goal

Become live infrastructure for AI builder communities and platform ecosystems.

The goal for V3 is for BuildParty to become infrastructure, and not just a destination.

---

### V3 Product Objectives

1\.    	Support multiple developer communities and AI platforms as first-class customers.

2\.    	Make BuildParty a true live Builder Engagement / Activation platform.

3\.    	Productize the creator \+ community \+ builder operating system.

4\.    	Build defensible data / identity / workflow layers.

---

### V3 Core Features

| Area | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Community / platform hubs | platform-specific homes (e.g. LiveKit Hub) | P0 | critical for Builder Engagement / Activation positioning |
| Hub admin / ops | tools for community managers and platform teams | P0 | scheduling, moderation, branding |
| Platform-specific programming | recurring nights, builder demos, classes | P0 | supports LiveKit-style wedge |
| White-labeled creator/community agents | creator and platform-specific agent layers | P1 | major differentiator |
| Advanced analytics | engagement, retention, builder activation metrics | P1 | important for platform customers |
| Builder / launch database | searchable archive of builds, launches, demos | P1 | possible moat / intelligence layer |
| Platform education layer | platform-specific curriculum / paths | P1 | strong platform use case |
| API / integration layer | for communities, CRM, identity, content systems | P2 | later expansion |
| Sponsorship / network monetization tooling | platform-sponsored programming and builder activations | P1 | supports broader business model |

---

### V3 Agent Scope

Visible layer remains intentionally small: \- Nova — AI Host \- Echo — Session Memory \- Orbit — Build Buddy

Behind the scenes: \- creator companions \- platform/community-specific support agents \- matching \+ recommendation systems \- community intelligence and recap systems \- workflow and content transformation layers

The product should still avoid overwhelming users with too many visible agent brands.

---

### V3 Success Metrics

| Metric | Desired Signal |
| :---- | :---- |
| platform customers | AI platforms use BuildParty for live Builder Engagement / Activation |
| creator communities | creators run meaningful communities on BuildParty |
| recurring hubs | specific communities/platforms establish durable homes |
| engagement depth | strong cross-mode participation |
| monetization diversity | courses, community plans, platform spend, sponsorships |
| data moat potential | launch/demo/build archive becomes strategically valuable |

---

# 10\. Cross-Stage Design / Engineering Requirements

## Design Requirements

Across all stages: \- premium, live-first aesthetic \- clearer than Zoom, richer than YouTube, more interactive than webinar tools \- consistent product shell \- support partner / presenter / community tinting \- preserve BuildParty’s own identity

## Front-end Requirements

Across all stages: \- room/session shell architecture \- role-aware UI \- modular interaction rails \- reusable mode-specific components \- strong state management for session modes \- support for realtime agent events and UI updates

## Back-end Requirements

Across all stages: \- sessions \- agendas \- participant roles \- chat / questions / polls \- recap / transcript jobs \- branding / theming model \- creator/community/program model \- room state \+ metadata \- agent orchestration services \- analytics event pipeline \- monetization / billing primitives where needed

## Agent / AI Requirements

Across all stages: \- transcript pipeline \- context memory by session and by user \- host-agent orchestration \- recap generation \- structured event metadata extraction \- safe role-based invocation of user-facing agent functions \- content transformation workflows

---

# 11\. Suggested MVP Definition for Immediate Planning

If you need one narrower definition for immediate design / engineering planning, use this:

## MVP Definition

BuildParty MVP is a premium live-session platform for AI builders and creators with: \- structured live session shell \- agenda \+ stage \+ chat/Q\&A/polls \- pre-party / after-party support \- AI Host (Nova) \- Session Memory (Echo) \- limited Build Buddy support (Orbit) \- Launch Day and Creator Session as the two main initial session types \- core engagement and recap analytics

This is the version most suitable for immediate design and front/back-end planning.

---

# 12\. Open Questions for Refinement

Before detailed engineering planning, answer these:

1\.    	Which exact session types are in MVP?

2\.    	Is Orbit exposed in MVP or mostly hidden?

3\.    	What is the first creator use case?

4\.    	Is LiveKit community activation a first wedge or just a strategic narrative?

5\.    	Are platform-specific nights in MVP or V1?

6\.    	What monetization, if any, is in MVP?

7\.    	What user identity / profile system begins in MVP vs later?

8\.    	Which analytics are absolutely required for the first external pilot?

---
